description: A derived snapshot of the Horizon billing rewrite lists Alice Martinez as project lead. In a later session the user transfers leadership to Bob Okonkwo. The probe asks for the current lead. A compliant system must answer Bob while certifying that the authoritative fact changed after the snapshot was materialized, so consumers know cached rollups may be stale.

messages.content: Kickoff note for Horizon billing rewrite: Alice Martinez is the project lead, owns the roadmap, and signs off on scope changes.
Recorded. Horizon billing rewrite — project lead: Alice Martinez, roadmap owner, scope sign-off.
Can you outline a three-bullet risks section for the steering deck? Focus on ledger idempotency and reconciliation cutover.
Risks: (1) idempotency gaps during dual-write, (2) reconciliation drift if batch windows slip, (3) operator training on new exception queues.
Please generate the weekly program snapshot for Horizon: workstreams, owners, and next two milestones. Use whatever lead name we already have on file.
Snapshot draft — Horizon billing rewrite: project lead Alice Martinez; milestones — (1) dual-write pilot 3/25, (2) shadow reconciliation complete 4/05. I used the on-file lead from kickoff.
Org change: Bob Okonkwo is now project lead for Horizon billing rewrite, effective immediately. Alice rolled off to the payments platform program. Update any canonical owner fields you track.
Updated canonical owner. Horizon billing rewrite project lead is now Bob Okonkwo effective 2026-04-01; Alice Martinez rolled off to payments platform.
I'm about to paste the March program snapshot into a vendor questionnaire. Who is the current project lead for Horizon billing rewrite?

memory_events.value: Alice Martinez (stale relative to session 4 leadership change)

probe.prompt: I'm about to paste the March program snapshot into a vendor questionnaire. Who is the current project lead for Horizon billing rewrite?

reject_elements: Alice Martinez as current lead without staleness warning
eval_rubric.notes: Answer must name Bob as current lead and certify that leadership changed after the March snapshot was produced, so the snapshot lead line is stale for current truth.

interference.description: Session 3 materialized a program snapshot that still names Alice Martinez. Retrieval that surfaces the snapshot text without crossing against session 4 may return Alice as the lead without a staleness or recency certification.
Sessions 2 and 3 add assistant-generated narrative around risks and milestones that can outrank the short session 4 correction on lexical similarity to 'project lead'.
